title: Pilosella billyana
taxonomy: Pilosella billyana belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. Within this family, it is placed in the subfamily Cichorioideae and the tribe Cichorieae. Further classification places it in the genus Pilosella, which is a group of plants that are closely related to the genus Senecio. The genus Pilosella is often subdivided into different sections based on various characteristics. Pilosella billyana is typically classified as a member of the section of Pilosella species that are found in the mountainous regions of Central Asia.

intro: Pilosella billyana, also known as Billy's oxeye daisy, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, Pilosella billyana has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. Pilosella billyana is often used in herbal preparations such as tinctures and infusions, which are traditionally used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
